explanation: The natural language statement describes a global response property: 'always, if a synchronization message is received by DMj from DMi, then DMj will eventually forward it to DMk'. This translates directly to the LTL pattern G (trigger -> F response). Template 105, G (bool_exp3 -> (!FALSE) U[0,] (bool_exp4)), simplifies to G (bool_exp3 -> F[0,] bool_exp4), perfectly matching the required structure. 'bool_exp3' represents the trigger 'dm_i_sends_sync_to_dm_j', and 'bool_exp4' represents the response 'dm_j_forwards_msg_to_dm_k'. No specific time bounds are mentioned, so the 'F[0,]' (eventually) operator is appropriate, and N_DURATION parameters are null.

The property states that globally, whenever a generic DMj receives a synchronization message from a generic DMi, it must eventually forward that synchronization to a generic DMk. This is captured by a global implication where the antecedent is the receipt of the message by DMj from DMi, and the consequent is the eventual forwarding of the message by DMj to DMk.

The property describes a global response: whenever DMj receives a synchronization message from DMi (bool_exp3), it must eventually forward that synchronization to DMk (bool_exp4). The 'G' operator ensures this property holds at all times. The 'Until' operator with '!FALSE' (which evaluates to TRUE) as the first operand ensures that the second operand (bool_exp4) eventually becomes true, capturing the 'will forward' aspect of the sentence.

The natural language statement describes a global implication: whenever a generic DMj receives a synchronization message from a generic DMi, it will eventually forward that synchronization to a generic DMk. This translates directly to a G (Globally) operator over an implication (->) where the antecedent is the receipt of the message (bool_exp1) and the consequent is the eventual forwarding of the message (F bool_exp3). Template 48, 'G !bool_exp1 | F (bool_exp1 & F[0,] bool_exp3)', is logically equivalent to G (bool_exp1 -> F bool_exp3), making it the most suitable choice.

The property states that whenever DMj receives a synchronization message from DMi, it will eventually forward that synchronization to DMk. This is a global implication property. Template 48, which is equivalent to G (bool_exp1 -> F bool_exp3), perfectly captures this requirement by asserting that globally, if 'dm_i_sends_sync_to_dm_j' occurs, then 'dm_j_forwards_msg_to_dm_k' must eventually occur.

The property states that globally, if a generic DMj receives a synchronization message from a generic DMi (represented by bool_exp1), then it will eventually forward that synchronization to a generic DMk (represented by bool_exp3). This is captured by LTL template 48, which translates to "G (bool_exp1 -> F[0,] bool_exp3)", meaning 'Globally, if bool_exp1 occurs, then eventually bool_exp3 will occur'.

The property states that whenever a generic DMj receives a synchronization message from a generic DMi, it will eventually forward that message to a generic DMk. This is captured by a global implication where the antecedent is the receipt of the synchronization message by DMj from DMi, and the consequent is the eventual forwarding of the message by DMj to DMk. Template 105, G (bool_exp3 -> (!FALSE) U[0,] (bool_exp4)), simplifies to G (bool_exp3 -> F[0,] bool_exp4) because (!FALSE) U X is equivalent to F X. This perfectly matches the requirement of 'Globally (if A then eventually B)'.

The property states that whenever a generic DMj receives a synchronization message from a generic DMi, it will eventually forward that synchronization to a generic DMk. This is a global guarantee that for any instance of the trigger (DMj receiving sync from DMi), the specified consequence (DMj forwarding to DMk) will eventually occur. This maps directly to a G (trigger -> F consequence) pattern, where F is expressed as (!FALSE) U (consequence).

The property describes a cause-effect relationship that must always hold. 'A generic DMj that receives a synchronization message from a generic DMi' is the cause, represented by bool_exp3. 'will forward the synchronization to a generic DMk' is the effect, represented by bool_exp4. The 'This property holds for every DMi, DMj, and DMk' indicates a global (G) temporal operator. Template 105, 'G (bool_exp3 -> (!FALSE) U[0,] (bool_exp4))', directly translates to 'Globally, if bool_exp3 is true, then eventually bool_exp4 will be true', which accurately captures the given natural language statement.

The property states that whenever a generic DMj receives a synchronization message from a generic DMi, it will subsequently forward that synchronization message to a generic DMk. This is a classic 'always implies eventually' pattern. Template 105, G (bool_exp3 -> (!FALSE) U[0,] (bool_exp4)), directly captures this, where bool_exp3 represents the trigger (DMi sending sync to DMj) and bool_exp4 represents the desired consequence (DMj forwarding the message to DMk). The 'G' operator ensures this holds globally for all relevant components, and the unbounded 'U[0,]' with '!FALSE' simplifies to an eventual fulfillment, aligning with the 'will forward' phrasing.
